originalTitle: How Keurig saved — and ruined — your coffee
summary: Before Keurig, the coffee in your office was almost certainly terrible. Old, burned, made by someone who would rather poorly eyeball than properly measure. Just altogether gross. After Keurig? You could make your own coffee, a cup at a time, exactly when you needed it. The single-cup brewer was an elegant solution to an extremely […]
content: Before Keurig, the coffee in your office was almost certainly terrible. Old, burned, made by someone who would rather poorly eyeball than properly measure. Just altogether gross. After Keurig? You could make your own coffee, a cup at a time, exactly when you needed it. The single-cup brewer was an elegant solution to an extremely common problem. At least, that’s how it started. On this episode of Version History, we dig into the history of the Keurig, and the ways in which the idea got vastly bigger than anyone expected. K-Cups and Keurig machines became ubiquitous in offices all across the US, then quickly began to invade our homes as well. But as David Pierce, Eater’s Melissa McCart, and creator and champion barista Morgan Eckroth discover, ultra-convenient coffee comes at a cost. To us, to the environment, and to the whole notion of “good coffee.” Keurig has spent years trying to reckon with all those things, while also trying to catch up to a coffee culture that might be leaving it behind. This is the fourth episode of the fourth season of Version History. If you haven’t yet checked out the story of the Harmony remote, the Roomba vacuum, or the Nest thermostat, make sure you catch up! It’s smart home season this season, and we’re having a blast. Here’s how to get every episode, and all our other fun stuff, as soon as it drops: * The Version History podcast feed * The Version History YouTube channel * Our TikTok and Instagram accounts If you’re a Verge subscriber, you can also get access to Version History (and all our other podcasts) with no ads. All you have to do is visit your account settings. If you want to know more about the early days of Keurig, here are some links to get you started: * From The Boston Globe: The inside story of Keurig’s rise to a billion-dollar coffee empire * From The Atlantic: How Bad Are K-Cups for the Environment? * Keurig caught making ‘inaccurate’ recycling claims about its coffee pods * Kill the K-Cup * From Mother Jones: Your Coffee Pods’ Dirty Secret * Keurig’s K-Brew and Chill coffee maker chills out drinks for pod lovers
chineseTitle: Keurig 如何拯救并毁掉你的咖啡
chineseSummary: 在 Keurig 之前，你办公室里的咖啡几乎肯定很糟糕。旧的、烧焦的，是由一个宁愿用肉眼观察也不愿正确测量的人制造的。简直太恶心了。继克里格之后？您可以在需要时自己煮咖啡，一次一杯。单杯啤酒机是一种优雅的解决方案，可以解决极其[...]
chineseContent: 在 Keurig 之前，你办公室里的咖啡几乎肯定很糟糕。旧的、烧焦的，是由一个宁愿用肉眼观察也不愿正确测量的人制造的。简直太恶心了。继克里格之后？您可以在需要时自己煮咖啡，一次一杯。单杯咖啡机是解决一个极其常见问题的优雅解决方案。至少，事情就是这样开始的。在这一集的版本历史中，我们深入探讨了 Keurig 的历史，以及这个想法如何变得比任何人预期的都要大。 K-Cups 和 Keurig 机器在美国各地的办公室中无处不在，然后也很快开始侵入我们的家庭。但正如 David Pierce、Eeater 的 Melissa McCart 和咖啡师创始人兼冠军 Morgan Eckroth 发现的那样，超级方便的咖啡是有代价的。对于我们，对于环境，对于“好咖啡”的整个概念。 Keurig 花了数年时间试图解决所有这些问题，同时也试图赶上可能会落后的咖啡文化。这是版本历史第四季的第四集。如果您还没有了解 Harmony 遥控器、Roomba 真空吸尘器或 Nest 恒温器的故事，请务必跟上！这个季节是智能家居季节，我们玩得很开心。以下是如何获取每一集以及我们所有其他有趣的内容，一旦发布： * 版本历史记录播客源 * 版本历史记录 YouTube 频道 * 我们的 TikTok 和 Instagram 帐户 如果您是 Verge 订阅者，您还可以访问版本历史记录（以及我们所有其他播客），没有广告。您所要做的就是访问您的帐户设置。如果您想了解更多关于 Keurig 早期的信息，请点击以下链接： * 来自《波士顿环球报》：
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
